comment: Synthetic recurrent / metastatic HNSCC, male 64, oropharyngeal SCC, HPV-negative (p16 IHC negative), heavy ex-smoker + alcohol, ECOG 1, recurrence at 14 months post-definitive cisplatin-RT, now with new lung metastases. PD-L1 CPS 35 by 22C3 IHC, no rapid progression / visceral crisis. CLINICAL TARGET: IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-PEMBRO-MONO-CPS-HIGH (KEYNOTE-048 Burtness Lancet 2019, mOS 14.9 mo, ~35% 24-mo OS, mDOR ~22 mo). ENGINE ROUTING: ALGO-HNSCC-RM-1L step 1 fires RF-HNSCC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION because the new_distant_metastasis trigger is satisfied (any_of clause does not require concurrent locoregional recurrence). Engine therefore selects IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-PEMBRO-MONO-CPS-HIGH as default (matching clinical target by route, even though the upstream trigger semantic is broader than the algorithm's free-text 'CPS ≥20' description suggests). KB-DRIFT NOTE: the algorithm's step-1 condition ('PD-L1 CPS ≥20 AND no rapid progression AND ECOG 0-2') is free-text and cannot be evaluated — the structured RF that drives the branch is RF-HNSCC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION, which fires on ANY new distant metastasis regardless of CPS. A patient with CPS 0 + new mets would also be routed to pembro-mono today; that is incorrect clinically. RF authoring gap flagged: a CPS-stratifying RedFlag is needed for HNSCC R/M 1L, and the transformation-progression RF should be tightened (require co-occurring locoregional / airway / carotid signal, not standalone new-mets).
